meta.title: G01 Engine Replacement Cost UK — Compare Quotes
meta.description: BMW X3 G01 engine replacement costs — B47, B57, B48, B58, S58 petrol and diesel. Real UK prices, M40i vs X3 M costs, and SUV‑specific guidance from 20+ vetted BMW specialists.

mainEntity.name: How much does a BMW X3 G01 engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned G01 engine typically costs £2,500–£16,000 supply only, depending on the engine — the B47 in the xDrive20d sits at £2,800–£4,500, the B58 in the M40i is £4,500–£7,500, and the S58 in the X3 M Competition is £9,000–£16,000. Fitting adds £650–£1,500. Use our calculator for a personalised figure.

mainEntity.name: Which BMW X3 G01 engine is the most reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: The B58B30O1/B in the M40i is the most reliable engine in the G01 range — BMW's modular straight-six is chain-driven, robust, and widely regarded as one of BMW's most dependable modern engines. On the diesel side, the B57D30B in the xDrive30d is the most reliable option — 265 PS, proven reliability, and strong economy. The B47 four-cylinder diesel is also reliable but has timing chain wear to watch for. The S58 in the X3 M is the most complex and expensive to maintain.

mainEntity.name: What are the common problems with the BMW X3 G01?

acceptedAnswer.text: On B48 petrol engines, coolant leaks from the cylinder head vent line (coolant hose) are the most common issue — the plastic hose can snap, dumping coolant. On B47 diesels, timing chain wear is the primary concern. On B57/B58 six-cylinders, cooling system maintenance (expansion tank/water pump) is important. On the S58 in the X3 M, turbocharger wear and cooling system demands are the main issues. The 48V mild-hybrid system is relatively new and may have integration issues.

mainEntity.name: Is the BMW X3 G01 reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: The G01 uses BMW's proven modular engine families — the B58 straight-six is widely regarded as one of BMW's most reliable engines. The B47 diesel is a significant improvement over the N47 with a front-mounted timing chain. The B48 is robust but has a documented cooling system weakness. The S58 in the X3 M requires rigorous maintenance. As with any BMW, service history is everything.

mainEntity.name: Which BMW X3 G01 engine should I avoid?

acceptedAnswer.text: We don't say 'avoid' outright — but a B48-powered sDrive20i or xDrive20i without documented cooling system history is a risk — the cylinder head vent line is a known weak point. A B47-powered diesel without timing chain inspection history is also a gamble. A well-maintained B58 M40i or B57 xDrive30d is the safest choice. If you must have an X3 M, budget for S58-specific maintenance.

mainEntity.name: How do I know if my G01 engine needs replacing?

acceptedAnswer.text: Coolant loss or overheating (B48), cold-start rattle (B47 timing chain), or loss of power/turbo whine (S58) are the key warning signs. On the B48, sudden coolant loss is the classic symptom of the cylinder head vent line failure. Use our diagnostic calculator for a personalised assessment.

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing a BMW X3 G01 engine, or should I scrap the car?

acceptedAnswer.text: Depends on the car's value. Against a well-kept £25,000 G01, a £4,500–£7,500 B58 recon is 18–30% of the car's value — safe. Against a tired £15,000 example, the same recon is 30–50% — still within our 70% BMW threshold. For an X3 M Competition, the economics are less favourable unless the car is low-mileage and well-specified. Always value the car first.

mainEntity.name: How long do BMW X3 G01 engines last?

acceptedAnswer.text: The B58 six-cylinder can exceed 200,000 miles with proper servicing — it's one of BMW's most durable modern engines. The B57 diesel six is similarly robust. The B47 four-cylinder diesel is capable of 150,000+ miles with timing chain maintenance. The S58 in the X3 M requires rigorous maintenance; with proper care, it can reach 150,000+ miles, but running costs are significant.

mainEntity.name: What is the difference between the G01 and F25 X3?

acceptedAnswer.text: The F25 (2010–2017) is the second-generation X3, while the G01 (2017–2024) is the third generation. The G01 is built on BMW's CLAR platform, offering improved interior space, technology, and driving dynamics. The G01 also introduced the B-series engine family (B48, B58, B47, B57) and the plug-in hybrid xDrive30e. The G45 (2024–present) is the successor. This page covers the G01 specifically — see the F25 page for the previous generation.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my BMW X3 G01 engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: Listed on the VIN plate and vehicle documentation; the engine code is also stamped on the cylinder block. Common G01 codes include B47D20A/B (sDrive18d/xDrive20d), B47D20O0 (xDrive20d), B47D20B (xDrive25d), B57D30B (xDrive30d/M40d), B48B20A (sDrive20i/xDrive20i/xDrive30e), B48B20B (xDrive30i), B58B30O1/B (M40i), and S58B30A (X3 M/X3 M Competition). Tell us your variant and registration date and we'll identify it for you.

hero.h1: BMW X3 G01 Engines — The Complete UK Guide
hero.subHeadline: Every engine fitted to the third-generation X3 G01 — from the 2.0-litre B48 four-cylinder petrol in the sDrive20i and xDrive30i, to the 3.0-litre B58 six-cylinder in the M40i, the S58-powered X3 M, and the full range of B47 and B57 diesel options for UK buyers. Real UK costs, honest reliability data, and the 2021 LCI updates that kept BMW's best-selling SUV competitive. Backed by 182+ real UK G01 enquiries in 2025 [BMW-VERIFIED].

engineDatabase.h2: BMW X3 G01 Engine Codes — The Complete Database
engineDatabase.subHeadline: Every engine fitted to the G01 X3 — from the entry-level sDrive18d to the range-topping X3 M Competition, including the plug-in hybrid xDrive30e.

confidenceScore.title: How confident are these ratings?
confidenceScore.text: The G01 uses BMW's proven modular engine families — B48 (four-cylinder petrol), B58 (six-cylinder petrol), S58 (M-division six-cylinder petrol), B47 (four-cylinder diesel), and B57 (six-cylinder diesel). The B58 is widely regarded as one of BMW's most reliable modern engines; the B47 is a significant improvement over the N47 with a front-mounted timing chain. The B48 is robust but has documented cooling system issues. The S58 in the X3 M is the most powerful and most expensive to maintain. All ratings are [PROVISIONAL] pending dedicated Engine Pages and verified enquiry data for this specific model.

overview.intro: The BMW X3 G01 is the third generation of BMW's compact luxury crossover SUV, unveiled in June 2017 with production commencing in August 2017. Built on BMW's CLAR platform, the G01 brought significant improvements in interior space, technology, and driving dynamics over its F25 predecessor. Designed by Calvin Luk, the G01 is manufactured at BMW's Spartanburg plant in the USA and Rosslyn plant in South Africa. The engine range includes B48 four-cylinder petrols (sDrive20i, xDrive20i, xDrive30i, and the xDrive30e plug-in hybrid), the B58 six-cylinder petrol in the M40i, the S58 in the X3 M and X3 M Competition, and B47/B57 diesels ranging from the sDrive18d to the M40d. A facelift (LCI) was introduced in 2021, and production ended in 2024.
overview.keyFacts: Platform: BMW CLAR platform • Predecessor: BMW X3 F25 • Successor: BMW X3 G45 • Sibling body styles in this generation: None (X4 G02 is a separate model line).
overview.marketIntelligenceLine: The BMW X3 G01 generated 182 real UK engine enquiries in 2025 [EM-VERIFIED], concentrated on the B47 diesel fours and B58 petrol sixes as these SUVs age out of warranty.

items.quote: The 3.0-litre straight-six diesel produces 265 PS and 620 Nm — effortless, refined, and with the kind of torque that makes the G01 a genuine long-distance cruiser. The B57 is chain-driven and proven across the BMW range. In the X3, it's the diesel you buy if you want six-cylinder refinement without M40d running costs.
items.whoItsFor: buyers who want effortless torque and strong economy from a six-cylinder diesel.

items.quote: The B58 is BMW's modular straight-six masterpiece — 360 PS, 500 Nm, silky smooth, and chain-driven with a robust bottom end. The B58 is widely regarded as one of BMW's most reliable modern engines. In the G01 X3, it's the engine that defines the performance SUV experience.
items.whoItsFor: buyers who want six-cylinder performance with proven reliability. (Model-wide: B58B30 is the benchmark across the BMW range — the G01 offers one of the best expressions of it in an SUV.)

items.quote: The B48 is a robust engine overall, but early production units can suffer from coolant leaks from the cylinder head vent line (coolant hose) — the plastic becomes brittle and snaps around 60,000 km, dumping coolant instantly. B48TU engines also have documented oil consumption issues.
items.whoItsFor: buyers who want entry-level X3 ownership with a budget for cooling system maintenance.

items.quote: The S58 is the high-performance M-division engine — up to 510 PS, twin-turbo, and complex. Turbocharger wear, cooling system demands, and M-specific component costs all add to the ownership burden. A full engine replacement sits at £8,000–£16,000.
items.whoItsFor: X3 M buyers who budget for high-end maintenance and have a specialist they trust.

items.quote: The xDrive20d's 2.0-litre four-cylinder diesel produces 190 PS and 400 Nm — the most efficient engine in the range, returning up to 50 mpg. The B47 is chain-driven with an accessible front-mounted chain — none of the N47's rear-chain anxiety. It's the X3 you can buy without diesel anxiety.
items.whoItsFor: high-mileage buyers who prioritise economy and reliability.

items.quote: The xDrive30e plug-in hybrid combines the B48B20A petrol engine with an electric motor for 292 PS and electric-only range. It's the most fuel-efficient petrol option for urban buyers and offers the lowest company car tax. The B48's cooling system issues are the only caveat.
items.whoItsFor: buyers who want petrol refinement with EV running costs for short journeys.

rows.ownershipVerdict: Best Diesel Value — efficient, chain accessible
Performance Diesel — 326 PS, complex but rewarding
Solid Choice — efficient, watch cooling system
Best Petrol Value — 252 PS, proven B48
Hybrid Choice — low tax, watch battery health
Best Petrol — 360 PS, the M40i's defining asset
Most Expensive — 510 PS, ultimate M costs

economicsRule.title: The G01 rule
economicsRule.text: A £4,500–£7,500 B58 recon against a £15,000–£45,000 G01 sits at 10–50% of the car's value — well within our 70% BMW threshold. Even the most expensive engine replacement (X3 M Competition S58 at £9,000–£16,000) is only 20–107% of a well-kept G01's value. On a sub-£15,000 high-mileage X3 M, that crosses our 70% threshold — we'd advise walking away. Value the car first. Extended warranty cover is strongly recommended, especially for S58 models and the 48V mild-hybrid system.

problems.title: B48 Coolant Leaks (Cylinder Head Vent Line)
problems.affectedModels: G01 sDrive20i, xDrive20i, xDrive30i, xDrive30e (B48 four-cylinder petrol).
problems.rootCause: The B48's cylinder head vent line (coolant hose) is made of plastic that becomes brittle with heat cycling — it can snap, dumping coolant instantly. This is the defining weakness of the B48 across the BMW range. B48TU engines also have documented oil consumption issues.
problems.recommendation: On any B48-powered G01 over 40,000 miles, have the coolant system inspected at every service — a £400–£800 preventative repair is far cheaper than the £1,000–£2,000 cooling system overhaul or engine replacement that follows an overheat event. The B48 is otherwise a robust engine.

cta.label: Compare B48 cooling system repair quotes for your G01 →

problems.title: B47 Timing Chain Wear (xDrive20d / sDrive18d)
problems.affectedModels: G01 sDrive18d, xDrive20d, xDrive25d (B47 four-cylinder diesel).
problems.rootCause: The B47's timing chain is front-mounted and significantly more durable than the N47's rear-mounted design. However, the chain relies on plastic tensioner rails that can soften and crack when exposed to diluted oil, allowing the chain to slap against the timing cover — creating a tell-tale cold-start rattle.
problems.recommendation: On any B47-powered G01, listen for cold-start rattle — a metallic clatter lasting 2–5 seconds after ignition. At the first sign, get the chain inspected immediately — an £800–£1,500 preventative repair is dramatically cheaper than the £2,800–£4,500 replacement that follows if ignored.

cta.label: Compare B47 engine prices for your G01 xDrive20d →

problems.title: B57 / B58 Cooling System Maintenance
problems.affectedModels: G01 xDrive30d, M40d, M40i (B57 and B58 six-cylinder).
problems.rootCause: The B57 and B58's cooling systems use plastic components that can become brittle with age — the expansion tank and water pump are the most common failure points. The B57 also has documented EGR cooler issues.
problems.recommendation: On any B57 or B58-powered G01 over 60,000 miles, have the cooling system inspected at every service — a £400–£800 preventative repair is far cheaper than the £1,000–£2,000 cooling system overhaul that follows. The B58 and B57 are otherwise robust engines.

cta.label: Compare B58/B57 cooling system repair quotes for your G01 →

problems.title: S58 Turbocharger & Cooling System Wear (X3 M / X3 M Competition)
problems.affectedModels: G01 X3 M, X3 M Competition (2019–2024, S58B30A).
problems.rootCause: The S58 is a high-performance engine with twin turbochargers and an M-specific cooling system. Turbochargers can suffer from oil leaks, worn bearings, or damaged turbine blades. The cooling system works harder than in standard models, increasing the risk of component fatigue.
problems.recommendation: On any S58-powered X3 M, have the turbochargers and cooling system inspected at 50,000 miles — a £1,500–£4,000 preventative service is dramatically cheaper than the £8,000–£16,000 engine replacement that follows if ignored. The X3 M is rewarding but requires serious maintenance commitment.

cta.label: Compare S58 engine prices for your X3 M →

replacementCosts.h2: G01 Engine Replacement Costs — By Engine and Condition

coreVariants.scopeNote: All variants listed are G01 X3 SUV only (2017–2024). The X3 M is designated F97 but is included on this page as it shares the G01 platform. The sDrive20i and sDrive18d are rear-wheel drive; all other variants are xDrive (all-wheel drive). The xDrive30e is a plug-in hybrid combining the B48B20A petrol engine with an electric motor for a combined 292 PS. The M40i uses the B58B30O1 (360 PS). The M40d uses the B57D30B (326 PS). The X3 M uses the S58B30A (480 PS) and the X3 M Competition produces 510 PS.

mostCommonFailures: 1. B48 coolant leaks (cylinder head vent line)
